classifications.rationale: Failure is not reproduced against the current commit on either of two consecutive runs. Without a reproduction we cannot classify it into a substantive category. Marking as `unknown_blocking` so it is governed and not silently dismissed; downstream H01 review may revisit if it re-emerges.

The test fixture (_init_repo) constructs a temp git repo without pinning commit.gpgsign locally. Host global git configuration leaks into the test environment. The production contract-impact analyzer is unaffected; only the test scaffolding is at fault.
Same root pattern as test_contract_impact_analysis — _init_git_repo helper lacks commit.gpgsign isolation. The production governed-autofix module under test is unaffected.

The runner's behavioral-proof subprocess (`_run_behavioral_tests`) shells out to a PATH-resolved `pytest`, which is a non-deterministic execution surface. PQX-equivalent execution authority requires reproducible invocation; the fix pins behavioral pytest invocations to the runner's interpreter (sys.executable -m pytest). The guard-policy gap is secondary: the policy already classified this kind of invocation as invalid but did not lock in determinism at execution time.
